title: A Night at the Traditional Market

all.en: On Friday evening, my cousin from Canada wanted to experience a traditional Taiwanese night market.
all.zh: 星期五傍晚，我從加拿大來的表弟想體驗傳統的台灣夜市。

all.en: We arrived just after sunset, when the lanterns were beginning to light up the narrow streets.
all.zh: 我們在日落後不久抵達，這時燈籠正開始照亮狹窄的街道。

all.en: The smell of grilled squid, stinky tofu, and fried chicken filled the warm evening air.
all.zh: 烤魷魚、臭豆腐與炸雞的香氣瀰漫在溫暖的夜晚空氣中。

all.en: My cousin was nervous about trying stinky tofu, but he bravely took one bite.
all.zh: 表弟對嘗試臭豆腐感到緊張，但他勇敢地咬了一口。

all.en: To his surprise, he said the flavor was much milder than the strong smell suggested.
all.zh: 令他驚訝的是，他說味道比那強烈的氣味溫和得多。

all.en: We waited fifteen minutes in line for a famous bubble tea shop with chewy tapioca balls.
all.zh: 我們在一家以Q彈珍珠聞名的珍珠奶茶店排了十五分鐘的隊。

all.en: A vendor demonstrated how to make oyster omelets on a huge, sizzling iron pan.
all.zh: 一位攤販示範如何在巨大、嘶嘶作響的鐵板上製作蚵仔煎。

all.en: We also played a small game where you try to catch goldfish with a paper net.
all.zh: 我們還玩了一個小遊戲，要用紙網撈金魚。

all.en: My cousin won a stuffed bear at a balloon-popping booth and felt very proud.
all.zh: 表弟在射氣球攤位贏得了一隻填充小熊，感到非常驕傲。

all.en: By the end of the night, our hands were full of snacks and small souvenirs.
all.zh: 到了夜晚結束時，我們手上滿是小吃與小紀念品。

all.en: He told me that the night market was the most exciting part of his entire trip.
all.zh: 他告訴我，夜市是他整趟旅程中最令人興奮的部分。

all.en: On our way out, we passed a fortune teller who offered to read my cousin's palm.
all.zh: 離開的路上，我們經過一位算命師，他主動要幫我表弟看手相。

all.en: The old woman predicted that my cousin would travel often and meet many interesting people.
all.zh: 那位老婦人預言我表弟會經常旅行，並遇見許多有趣的人。

all.en: My cousin laughed but secretly seemed pleased with the kind words she had spoken.
all.zh: 我表弟笑了，但似乎暗自對她說的善意話語感到開心。

all.en: We sat on plastic stools and ordered a final bowl of warm peanut soup with rice cakes.
all.zh: 我們坐在塑膠凳上，最後又點了一碗溫熱的花生湯加麻糬。

all.en: Nearby, an elderly man played old Taiwanese songs on a small wooden flute.
all.zh: 附近，一位老先生用小木笛吹奏著古老的台語歌曲。

all.en: My cousin recorded a short video of the music so he could remember the atmosphere later.
all.zh: 我表弟錄了一段短片捕捉這段音樂，好讓自己日後回憶起當時的氛圍。

all.en: On the taxi ride home, he reviewed all the photos he had taken throughout the night.
all.zh: 回程的計程車上，他翻看著整晚拍下的所有照片。

all.en: He told his parents on a video call that he wanted to bring them next year.
all.zh: 他在視訊通話中告訴父母，他想明年帶他們一起來。

all.en: Before flying back to Canada, he bought a small night market guidebook as a souvenir.
all.zh: 飛回加拿大前，他買了一本小小的夜市指南當作紀念品。

all.en: He promised to recommend Taiwanese night markets to all his friends and coworkers.
all.zh: 他答應會向所有的朋友與同事推薦台灣的夜市。

all.en: A year later, my cousin returned to Taiwan with both of his parents during summer vacation.
all.zh: 一年後，我表弟趁暑假帶著他的父母一起回到台灣。

all.en: He had carefully circled six different night markets in his small guidebook before arriving.
all.zh: 他抵達前已在小指南上仔細圈出六個不同的夜市。

all.en: His mother loved the colorful fruit stalls selling fresh mangoes, pineapples, and dragon fruit.
all.zh: 他媽媽很喜歡賣芒果、鳳梨與火龍果的繽紛水果攤。

all.en: His father, a retired teacher, was fascinated by the traditional calligraphy stalls near the entrance.
all.zh: 他爸爸是退休老師，對入口附近的傳統書法攤位非常著迷。

all.en: We took them to a smaller, less crowded market in Tainan known for its old-fashioned snacks.
all.zh: 我們帶他們去台南一個比較小、人潮較少的市場，以古早味小吃聞名。

all.en: My cousin's mother insisted on buying matching aprons for the whole family as souvenirs.
all.zh: 我表弟的媽媽堅持要為全家人買成套的圍裙作為紀念品。

all.en: His father practiced ordering food in Mandarin and made the stall owners laugh kindly.
all.zh: 他爸爸練習用中文點餐，逗得攤販們親切地笑了。

all.en: On their last evening, we visited a quiet riverside market with delicious aboriginal Taiwanese dishes.
all.zh: 在他們最後一晚，我們去了一個寧靜的河邊市場，吃了美味的原住民料理。

all.en: My cousin recorded everything on his phone to share with his coworkers back in Toronto.
all.zh: 我表弟用手機錄下一切，回多倫多後要分享給同事。

all.en: Before flying home, his parents thanked me for showing them the true heart of Taiwan.
all.zh: 飛回家前，他的父母感謝我讓他們看見台灣真正的精神。

all.en: After his parents returned to Canada, my cousin began studying Mandarin every weekday evening.
all.zh: 父母回到加拿大後，我表弟開始每個週間晚上學習中文。

all.en: He hoped to read his small Taiwanese guidebook fluently before his next planned visit.
all.zh: 他希望在下次計畫造訪前，能流利閱讀那本小小的台灣指南。

all.en: Six months later, he sent me photos of him cooking oyster omelets in his Toronto kitchen.
all.zh: 六個月後，他傳來他在多倫多廚房裡煎蚵仔煎的照片。

all.en: His coworkers loved the Taiwanese snacks so much that they asked him to host a weekend party.
all.zh: 他的同事們非常喜歡台灣小吃，因此請他舉辦一場週末派對。

all.en: He filled his apartment with paper lanterns and played the recorded flute music from our market.
all.zh: 他用紙燈籠裝飾整間公寓，並播放他在夜市錄下的木笛音樂。

all.en: His mother flew over from another city to help him prepare authentic bubble tea for the guests.
all.zh: 他媽媽從另一個城市飛來，協助他為賓客準備正宗的珍珠奶茶。

all.en: Several Canadian friends asked him to plan a group trip to Taiwan the following summer.
all.zh: 好幾位加拿大朋友請他規劃隔年夏天的台灣團體旅行。

all.en: He created a detailed travel plan covering eight different night markets across the whole island.
all.zh: 他擬定了一份詳盡的旅行計畫，涵蓋全島八個不同的夜市。

all.en: When the group finally arrived in Taipei, he proudly served as their experienced tour guide.
all.zh: 當這群朋友終於抵達台北時，他驕傲地擔任他們經驗豐富的導遊。

all.en: That fortune teller's prediction had quietly come true through every meal and every friendly conversation.
all.zh: 那位算命師的預言透過每一頓飯與每一段親切對話悄悄實現了。

all.en: After the group trip, my cousin decided to take a six-month break from his Toronto office job.
all.zh: 那趟團體旅行後，我表弟決定從他的多倫多辦公室工作中休假六個月。

all.en: He moved to Tainan temporarily to study Mandarin and Taiwanese cooking at a local cultural institute.
all.zh: 他暫時搬到台南，到當地一所文化學院學習中文與台菜料理。

all.en: He shared a small apartment with two friendly classmates from Korea and the Philippines.
all.zh: 他和兩位來自韓國與菲律賓的友善同學合租了一間小公寓。

all.en: Every weekend, he visited a different night market and wrote detailed reviews in his personal blog.
all.zh: 每個週末，他造訪一個不同的夜市，並在個人部落格撰寫詳盡的評論。

all.en: His blog grew rapidly, attracting readers from Canada, Australia, and several European countries.
all.zh: 他的部落格快速成長，吸引了來自加拿大、澳洲與數個歐洲國家的讀者。

all.en: A Taiwanese tourism office invited him to make short videos promoting lesser-known traditional markets.
all.zh: 台灣的觀光單位邀請他製作短片，推廣較不為人知的傳統市場。

all.en: He created a popular video series featuring elderly stall owners sharing their family recipes openly.
all.zh: 他製作了一個熱門影集，採訪年長攤主，請他們公開分享家族食譜。

all.en: The old flute player from our first market night appeared as a special guest in episode three.
all.zh: 我們第一次夜市之夜遇見的那位老木笛師，作為特別來賓出現在第三集中。

all.en: He told my cousin he had been waiting decades for someone to truly listen to his old songs.
all.zh: 他告訴我表弟，他已等了數十年，才有人真正用心聆聽他的老歌。

all.en: That sentence became the title of my cousin's first published book about life in Taiwan.
all.zh: 這句話成為我表弟第一本關於台灣生活著作的書名。

all.en: My cousin's first book sold steadily and was reprinted three times within its quiet opening year.
all.zh: 我表弟的第一本書銷售穩定，在其安靜的首發年內就再版了三次。

all.en: Schools across the island added the book to their weekend cultural reading recommendation lists.
all.zh: 全島的學校將這本書納入週末文化閱讀推薦書單。

all.en: The old flute player passed away peacefully the following winter, surrounded by his now-numerous fans.
all.zh: 那位老木笛師在隔年冬天安詳離世，身旁圍繞著如今眾多的粉絲。

all.en: My cousin organized a memorial concert at our beloved traditional market on a clear autumn evening.
all.zh: 我表弟在一個晴朗的秋夜，於我們深愛的傳統市場舉辦了一場追思音樂會。

all.en: Young musicians performed his old songs while elderly market vendors quietly wiped their tearful eyes.
all.zh: 年輕音樂家演奏他的老歌，年長的市場攤商默默擦拭著流淚的眼睛。

all.en: My cousin began writing a second book, this time about the kind elderly noodle vendor next door.
all.zh: 我表弟開始撰寫第二本書，這次的主題是隔壁善良的年長麵攤老闆。

all.en: The vendor shared sixty years of recipe stories during quiet afternoon interviews between busy customers.
all.zh: 麵攤老闆在繁忙顧客之間的寧靜午後訪談中，分享了六十年的食譜故事。

all.en: His daughter agreed to keep the noodle stall running for at least one more entire generation.
all.zh: 他的女兒答應至少再經營麵攤一整個世代。

all.en: She thanked my cousin publicly for helping her father's quiet life finally feel truly seen and honored.
all.zh: 她公開感謝我表弟，協助她父親安靜的一生終於真正被看見並受到尊敬。

all.en: Our night market had quietly become a living archive of gentle stories carried across many generations.
all.zh: 我們的夜市已悄悄成為一座承載溫柔故事的活檔案，跨越無數世代。

all.en: City officials proposed installing small bronze plaques near each historic stall mentioned in his books.
all.zh: 市府官員提議在他書中提及的每個歷史攤位旁設置小型銅製紀念牌。

all.en: Each plaque told the vendor's name, opening year, and one signature dish in three different languages.
all.zh: 每塊紀念牌以三種不同語言介紹攤主姓名、開業年份與一道招牌菜。

all.en: Tourists began walking a self-guided heritage route through the market using maps printed by volunteers.
all.zh: 觀光客開始拿著志工印製的地圖，沿著自助式文化路線走訪市場。

all.en: My cousin received a community service award from the mayor during a warm spring ceremony outdoors.
all.zh: 我表弟在一場溫暖的春季戶外典禮中，從市長手中獲頒社區服務獎。

all.en: He humbly dedicated the award to every elderly vendor who had ever shared a story with him.
all.zh: 他謙遜地把獎獻給每一位曾與他分享故事的年長攤主。

all.en: On a rainy evening, he proposed to a kind Taiwanese teacher he had met at the cooking institute.
all.zh: 在一個下雨的夜晚，他向他在烹飪學院認識的一位溫柔台灣老師求婚。

all.en: She joyfully accepted while sharing a final bowl of peanut soup at our familiar plastic stools.
all.zh: 她在我們熟悉的塑膠凳上分享最後一碗花生湯時，喜悅地答應了。

all.en: Their wedding was held under lanterns at the same market where his first cultural journey began.
all.zh: 他們的婚禮在燈籠下舉行，地點正是他第一次文化之旅開始的那個市場。

all.en: Years later, their children would run between the stalls, greeting every vendor by their first name.
all.zh: 多年後，他們的孩子會在攤位間奔跑，親切地以名字稱呼每位攤主。

all.en: The market continued to thrive, carrying the gentle echoes of every conversation that ever happened there.
all.zh: 市場持續興盛，承載著曾在那裡發生過的每一段對話的溫柔回響。

all.en: Their oldest son inherited a love of recording elderly voices and began carrying a small camera everywhere.
all.zh: 他們的長子繼承了記錄長者聲音的熱忱，並開始隨身攜帶一台小相機。

all.en: He launched a podcast featuring monthly interviews with vendors from markets all across the southern coast.
all.zh: 他開設了一個 Podcast 節目，每月訪問南部沿海各市場的攤商。

all.en: The podcast quickly attracted listeners who craved gentle, unhurried stories during their busy commutes.
all.zh: 這個 Podcast 迅速吸引了渴望在忙碌通勤中聆聽溫柔慢敘故事的聽眾。

all.en: A retired sound engineer volunteered to help him restore old market recordings from the previous century.
all.zh: 一位退休的錄音工程師自願協助他修復上個世紀留下的市場舊錄音。

all.en: Together they discovered tapes featuring market songs that no one in the family had heard before.
all.zh: 他們一同發現了家族中無人聽過的市場歌謠錄音帶。

all.en: Those tapes were carefully digitized and placed in a free online archive open to every curious visitor.
all.zh: 那些錄音帶被仔細數位化，存入一個對每位好奇訪客免費開放的線上檔案庫。

all.en: His younger sister composed gentle modern arrangements of those old tunes for a community choir.
all.zh: 他的妹妹為一個社區合唱團，把那些老調編成溫柔的現代版本。

all.en: The choir performed at the market's hundredth anniversary celebration on a crisp autumn weekend afternoon.
all.zh: 在一個清爽的秋日週末午後，合唱團於市場百年慶典上演出。

all.en: Listeners said they could almost hear the original flute player smiling somewhere among the lanterns above.
all.zh: 聽眾說，他們幾乎能聽見原本那位木笛師在上方燈籠之間微笑的聲音。

all.en: Our family understood then that one curious night long ago had truly become a living legacy.
all.zh: 我們家族於是明白，許久以前那個充滿好奇的夜晚，已真正成為一份活著的傳承。

all.en: Last winter, the oldest son and his wife began bringing their newborn baby to the market every Saturday morning.
all.zh: 去年冬天，大兒子和他的妻子開始每週六早晨帶著新生的寶寶來市場。

all.en: Vendors crowded gently around the stroller, offering tiny dumpling samples and warm honey biscuits as small gifts.
all.zh: 攤販們溫柔地圍著嬰兒車，獻上小份餃子試吃與溫熱的蜂蜜餅乾作為小禮物。

all.en: The baby fell asleep to the familiar sounds of vendors haggling kindly and steel pans sizzling in the morning air.
all.zh: 寶寶在攤販溫和的議價聲與晨間平底鍋滋滋作響的聲音中沉沉睡去。

all.en: One vendor knitted a soft hat shaped like a tiny dumpling and tucked it into the stroller as a baby gift.
all.zh: 一位攤販織了一頂造型像小餃子的軟帽，把它塞進嬰兒車裡作為禮物。

all.en: His parents framed a photograph of the baby wearing that hat beside their original wedding market portrait.
all.zh: 他父母把寶寶戴著那頂帽子的照片裱框，放在他們最初在市場拍的婚禮照片旁邊。

all.en: Local children began calling the baby their tiny market cousin and lined up to make him laugh each weekend.
all.zh: 當地的孩子們開始稱寶寶為他們的小小市場表弟，每個週末都排隊逗他笑。

all.en: By his first birthday, the entire market gathered in the central courtyard for a small celebration potluck.
all.zh: 到了他週歲時，整個市場齊聚在中央庭院舉辦小型百樂餐慶祝。

all.en: Each vendor brought one childhood dish from their own family, layered in mismatched bowls on a long wooden table.
all.zh: 每位攤販都帶了一道來自家族的童年料理，用不成套的碗盛裝，擺在一張長木桌上。

all.en: The retired flute-recording engineer played a soft lullaby on a tiny bamboo flute he had carved from market wood.
all.zh: 那位退休的木笛錄音工程師，用他從市場木材中刻成的小竹笛吹奏了一首輕柔的搖籃曲。

all.en: Watching the baby clap along, I knew the market's gentle song would carry on into another lifetime of laughter.
all.zh: 看著寶寶跟著拍手，我知道市場那首溫柔的歌將會延續到另一個充滿歡笑的世代。

all.en: We squeezed through the crowd, following the scent of sizzling skewers.
all.zh: 我們擠過人群，循著滋滋作響的串燒香味前進。

all.en: My cousin tried stinky tofu and was surprised that he actually liked it.
all.zh: 我表親嚐了臭豆腐，意外發現自己其實很喜歡。

all.en: A game stall offered a prize if you could knock down all the cans.
all.zh: 一個遊戲攤位說，把所有罐子都打倒就能贏得獎品。

all.en: We shared a bubble tea while watching a vendor flip oyster omelets.
all.zh: 我們一邊共喝一杯珍珠奶茶，一邊看攤販翻炒蚵仔煎。

all.en: Bargaining politely, I got a phone case for half the asking price.
all.zh: 我禮貌地殺價，用一半的開價買到了一個手機殼。

all.en: The vendors shouted their prices over the cheerful evening noise.
all.zh: 攤販們在熱鬧的夜晚喧鬧聲中高喊著價錢。

all.en: We sat on plastic stools and slurped noodles under a string of lights.
all.zh: 我們坐在塑膠凳上，在一串燈泡下嗦著麵。

all.en: My cousin filmed a short video to show his friends back home.
all.zh: 我表親拍了段短片，要給家鄉的朋友看。

all.en: Even after we were full, the sweet smell of waffles tempted us.
all.zh: 就算吃飽了，鬆餅的甜香還是誘惑著我們。

all.en: We left with full stomachs and bags of snacks for the next day.
all.zh: 我們心滿意足地離開，還帶了幾袋隔天要吃的零食。
